headline: ProMedica / Medical Mutual network dispute — resolved with new multi-year agreement (November 2025)
paragraphs: ProMedica and Medical Mutual finalized a new multi-year network participation agreement on November 14, 2025, ending a months-long dispute that had threatened to remove ProMedica from Medical Mutual's in-network provider list for the Toledo area. The agreement applies to all Medical Mutual and Medical Mutual-Paramount insurance products, including employer-sponsored health plans, commercial insurance, ACA marketplace plans, and Medicare Advantage. ProMedica remains in-network for all affected members.

The dispute originated in mid-2025 when Medical Mutual notified members that ProMedica could be dropped if a new contract was not reached before 2026. The November 14 resolution — reported simultaneously by 13abc, The Blade, and WTOL — closed that risk before it materialized. The story is closed out as of May 31, 2026.
why_it_matters: ProMedica is one of downtown Toledo's largest employers and a anchor of the city's healthcare economy. The resolution removes insurance-network turbulence as a workforce-attraction headwind — employees of Toledo's employers who carry Medical Mutual coverage can access ProMedica facilities without navigating out-of-network costs.

sources.title: The Blade — ProMedica remains in-network for Medical Mutual, Paramount customers (Nov. 14, 2025)
WTOL — ProMedica finalizes deal with Medical Mutual to continue in-network access for thousands of patients
WTOL — Medical Mutual may drop ProMedica as in-network provider (original dispute reporting)
